# Deep Dive: AI Synthetic Supply

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

## Core idea

AI reduces the cost of producing supply: text, music, images, personas, reviews, ads, comments, and videos. Manufactured traction emerges when synthetic supply is paired with synthetic or distorted demand.

## Synthetic supply examples

- AI-generated songs.
- AI-generated review drafts.
- AI-generated influencers.
- AI-generated scam ads.
- AI-generated content farms.
- AI-generated comments or replies.
- AI-generated profile photos and bios.

## Risk pattern

AI can create content faster than trust systems can verify it. When distribution systems reward volume, cheap synthetic supply can crowd out genuine work.

## Strongest current examples

- DOJ Michael Smith case: AI songs plus bot streams.
- Deezer AI music upload flood.
- AP / Google Ads Safety Report: AI-scale scam ads and AI-scale enforcement.
- Meta original-content updates: unoriginal content and impersonation.

## Research question

How does the cost collapse of content production change the economics of deception?
